executive_brief: CourseHub API is a course management system providing secure academic workflows, featuring instructor-led content creation and student progress tracking. Hosted as a FastAPI-based service, it implements a role-based access control pattern to isolate student data and enforce strict course ownership and enrollment dependencies.

architectural_constraints: Course deletion: Forbidden if active enrollments exist
Data Isolation: Students strictly forbidden from updating other students' progress (403)
Progress logic: Progress == 100 must trigger auto-set of completed_at
Testing constraint: Zero mocking of PostgreSQL; requires real test instance
Response structure: Consistent global APIResponse envelope across all endpoints

maturity_assessment: The specifications demonstrate high structural integrity with all core phases from setup to polish clearly mapped. While a minor gap exists regarding explicit edge-case documentation for the email service, the comprehensive task list and defined success criteria render the project READY for execution.
critical_dependencies: RESEND_API_KEY: Mandatory environment variable required at startup (raises ConfigError if missing)
JWT: Core dependency for auth flow and role-based access control
PostgreSQL: Required for persistence and real-instance integration testing
Referential Integrity: Strict dependency between Course and Enrollment entities for deletion constraints
Phase Sequence: Linear dependency chain from Setup -> Auth -> Email before domain implementation
